headerTooltips: Key organizations, institutions, government entities, funders, and other stakeholders collaborating on initiatives
Grouped approaches that organize multiple related activities into coherent intervention areas
Evidence that directly demonstrates an activity occurred as planned
Preliminary results that directly follow outputs, representing initial changes in opportunities, capacity, and incentives among target populations and community systems
Changes that build upon short-term outcomes, representing shifts in behaviors and community conditions
Sustained, transformational changes in community conditions and quality of life that represent the strategy's ultimate goals

Mixed-income Rental Housing Development.label: Mixed-income Rental Housing Development
Mixed-income Rental Housing Development.details: In collaboration with Pennrose, REACH Riverside’s housing developer partner, this strategy delivers high-quality, mixed-income rental housing through multi-phase apartment complex development (Imani Village, which is being built over the course of nine phases). The development prioritizes legacy Riverside residents, offering them first right to occupancy. By intentionally mixing deeply subsidized, affordable, and market-rate units, this approach creates economically integrated communities with the aim of breaking cycles of concentrated poverty, while ensuring that existing Riverside households can remain in the neighborhood.

Commercial Corridor Revitalization.label: Commercial Corridor Revitalization
Commercial Corridor Revitalization.details: Leveraging funding from a Choice Neighborhood Implementation grant (in addition to a variety of other ongoing funding sources) and a wide range of partnerships across Riverside, this strategy reclaims underutilized and blighted property across Riverside, converting land into community-serving assets. This comprehensive redevelopment encompasses new community facilities, single-family housing development, and critical community resources including food retail, food production infrastructure, community gathering spaces, and mixed-use facilities. By RESTORING vacant lots and deteriorating buildings into vibrant community spaces and businesses, this strategy improves the physical environment while creating wealth-building opportunities for residents through community business ownership and employment.
Commercial Corridor Revitalization.activities: Real Estate Strategy to Obtain Racial Equity (RESTORE) program

Wealth Creation through Home Ownership.label: Wealth Creation through Home Ownership
Wealth Creation through Home Ownership.details: This strategy creates pathways to homeownership and generational wealth by developing for-sale housing units through multiple development models. The Community Land Trust model ensures permanent affordability by separating land ownership from home ownership, allowing families to build equity through home appreciation while keeping housing costs accessible for future generations. Combined with homeownership preparation services including credit counseling, financial literacy, and down payment assistance navigation, this strategy removes traditional barriers to homeownership for low- and moderate-income families while prioritizing legacy residents and directly countering displacement pressures.
Wealth Creation through Home Ownership.activities:
Real Estate Strategy to Obtain Racial Equity (RESTORE) program townhome development
Community Land Trust Formation
Home ownership preparation program

Housing Support Services.label: Housing Support Services
Housing Support Services.details: This strategy provides comprehensive wrap-around services to ensure housing stability across Riverside. Families receive individualized support, including financial literacy training; rental, utility and mortgage assistance; and home ownership preparation programs. Data from family strengths and needs assessments informs ongoing service navigation support and connections with tailored resources aligned with family goals. This holistic approach addresses the multifaceted barriers to housing stability—from managing rental delinquency to improving the quality of neighborhood homes—while preparing families for the transition from renting to homeownership and building the financial acumen necessary for long-term stability and wealth creation.
Housing Support Services.activities:
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program
Client management
Financial coaching
Home improvement and home ownership support

Early Learning.label: Early Learning
Early Learning.details: Kingswood Community Center’s Early Learning Academy provides Montessori-based early childhood education for children ages 0-5, ensuring comprehensive developmental support through regular assessments using standardized tools to track children's progress toward kindergarten readiness. The program integrates music education through community partnerships, providing play-based, culturally responsive instruction that enhances self-regulation, social-emotional well-being, and literacy skills. The strategy combines high-quality Montessori curriculum with health and dental screenings, developmental monitoring, and connections to special education services when needed.

Educator and School Support.label: Educator and School Support
Educator and School Support.details: This strategy strengthens the local educator workforce and enhances school-based support through multiple complementary initiatives. University-based teacher residency programs provide high-quality Montessori training and certification for early learning teachers, ensuring they have the specialized skills needed for effective instruction while focusing on family engagement, classroom management, and supporting English language learners and students with special needs. Shared services alliances expand educator resources across multiple early learning centers by creating shared substitute teacher pools and coordinating access to behavioral health services, health screenings, and other supports that individual centers struggle to provide independently.
Educator and School Support.activities:
University of Delaware Montessori Teacher Residency
Child Development Associates training
Early Education Associates (CDA) and Bachelor's Degree programs
Wilmington Early Childhood Shared Services

K-12 Education.label: K-12 Education
K-12 Education.details:
This strategy provides rigorous academic programming for students from kindergarten through high school graduation through EastSide Charter School (K-8th grade) and Colonial School District (K-12th grade) schools. EastSide Charter School offers lower school (K-3rd grade), upper school (4th-8th grade), honors programming, and STEM-focused instruction to build toward college and career readiness. For high school students and specialized services, the strategy relies on partnership with public high schools as part of Colonial School District. 

Supplementing core academics, student navigators work directly with chronically absent or academically struggling students, offering personalized support to address barriers to learning and build confidence through regular meetings and goal-setting, while family navigators work alongside families to help them navigate challenges, identify opportunities, and connect with community resources through personalized success plans. Outside of school hours, students have access to additional services and support through The Warehouse (see Out of School Time strategy).
K-12 Education.activities:
K-12 Education
Goal Planning for Success (GPS) program

Out of School Time.label: Out of School Time
Out of School Time.details:
This comprehensive strategy provides academically enriching, developmentally appropriate programming during non-school hours for children, youth and young adults. Before and after care serves elementary-aged children, while summer camp programming serves 5-12 year olds, including a range of academic enrichment, sports and recreation, performance and visual arts, social-emotional learning, field trips, and nutritious meals. 

Youth and young adults (ages 13-24) from all area middle and high schools, meanwhile, have access to a variety of programs (Recreation, Education, Arts, Careers, and Health) during afternoon and evening hours at The Warehouse. The Warehouse programming aims to support youth and young adults' growth by providing safe, engaging, and development-focused opportunities that equip youth with the skills, confidence, and connections needed to succeed academically, socially, and professionally, while fostering a supportive community where they can explore their interests, build meaningful relationships, and access comprehensive enrichment experiences that nurture personal development, strengthen life skills, and prepare them for future education and career pathways.
Out of School Time.activities:
Summer Camp
Before and After Care
Membership at The Warehouse
Reaching and Investing in Youth for Sustainable Employment (RISE): In School Youth (ISY) program
RISE: Out-of-School Youth (OSY) program

Family Support and Services.label: Family Support and Services
Family Support and Services.details: This strategy recognizes that student success depends on family stability and engagement, providing comprehensive support to parents and caregivers. Family services coordinators conduct strengths and needs assessments with families enrolled in early learning and youth programs, developing partnership agreements and maintaining regular contact to provide service navigation support. For EastSide Charter School families facing challenges such as chronic absenteeism or academic struggle, family navigators work alongside them to navigate challenges, identify opportunities, and connect with community resources through personalized success plans. Parent councils and engagement events create opportunities for families to connect with one another and with program staff through social events and educational workshops.
Family Support and Services.activities:
Family Services: Parent Council and Family Services Coordinator
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program
Goal Planning for Success program

Post-secondary and Adult Education.label: Post-secondary and Adult Education
Post-secondary and Adult Education.details: This strategy provides comprehensive pathways for youh and young adults who are not currently enrolled in school to obtain their GED or career certifications. Specifically, the RISE: OSY program combines intensive durability training—covering career readiness, financial literacy, and customer service—with GED or career certification programs offered through community college partnerships. Participants receive individualized service strategies, ongoing case management, and support navigating barriers to education completion, with tracking of measurable skills gain, credential obtainment, and placement into post-secondary education, training, employment or military service.
Post-secondary and Adult Education.activities:
Reaching and Investing in Youth for Sustainable Employment (RISE): Out-of-School Youth program
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program

Health Access and Resource Coordination.label: Health Access and Resource Coordination
Health Access and Resource Coordination.details: This strategy ensures residents have access to comprehensive, affordable physical and mental health services in addition to needed community resources. Health services are embedded in community facilities, bringing specialized care directly into the community and eliminating transportation and access barriers that often prevent families from seeking care. Community Events, such as an annual Holiday Toy and Coat Drive, connect community members with needed resources. Building on this, food access programs address food insecurity through a free pantry, community refrigerators, urban agriculture, and snack distribution strategically placed at key venues such as The Warehouse. Additionally at The Warehouse, mentoring supports and mental health services support youth and young adults in reaching their full potential.
Health Access and Resource Coordination.activities:
Food Equity Delivers Unlimited Potential (FED-UP) program
ChristianaCare Health Services
Nemours Health Services
Mental health services
English Lessons Youth Mentoring Program
Community Events
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program
Membership at The Warehouse
Reaching and Investing in Youth for Sustainable Employment (RISE): In-school Youth (ISY) program
RISE: Out-of-school Youth (OSY) program
Goal Planning for Success (GPS) program
Jimmy Jenkins Senior Center

Senior Support.label: Senior Support
Senior Support.details: This strategy provides comprehensive programming that maximizes independence and enhances quality of life for seniors. The senior center offers daily nutritious meals, social and physical health activities including exercise and fitness classes, mental health support, arts and crafts programming, games, field trips, and health information sessions and screenings. By addressing seniors' financial, medical, and social needs in an integrated setting, the strategy creates a supportive community that combats isolation while promoting active aging. The center serves as both a direct service provider and a connection point to additional community resources, ensuring seniors can navigate the complex landscape of aging services and supports.

Building a Vibrant, Socially Cohesive Co.label: Building a Vibrant, Socially Cohesive Community
Building a Vibrant, Socially Cohesive Co.details: This strategy weaves together multiple initiatives that strengthen social bonds, increase community participation, and build collective efficacy across the neighborhood. Community events including seasonal celebrations, movie screenings, holiday initiatives, and ceremonial gatherings create regular opportunities for celebration and belonging. Community ambassadors promote events and strengthen neighbor-to-neighbor connections. Volunteer management mobilizes community members and external supporters to staff food distribution, events, and special projects, building a culture of civic engagement. Community tours provide transparent visibility into revitalization efforts, building trust and investment. At The Warehouse, youth and young adults build relationships and community connectivity through sports and recreation, arts programming, health education, and educational workshops. Together, initiatives under this strategy contribute to an engaged and integrated community.
Building a Vibrant, Socially Cohesive Co.activities:
Membership at The Warehouse
WRK Group Tours
Community Events
Volunteer Management
Village Impact Promoters (VIP)
Partnership Development
English Lessons Youth Mentoring Program

Financial Management Support.label: Financial Management Support
Financial Management Support.details:
This strategy provides intensive, ongoing support to help residents, including youth and young adults, achieve financial stability and build wealth. For youth and young adults, RISE offers eight weeks of durable skills training and personal/career development followed by a four-week work-related experience or certification, depending on participant needs and goals. RISE programs for both in-school and out-of-school youth integrate financial literacy as a core component of durable skills training, ensuring young people develop money management capabilities alongside employment readiness. 

Through EMPOWER, participants complete self-sufficiency assessments at regular intervals to track progress across multiple dimensions of financial wellbeing, while developing and working toward individualized service plans that address their specific goals and barriers. One-on-one financial coaching focuses on improving credit scores, reducing personal debt, increasing savings, and working toward asset goals including motor vehicle and home purchases. The strategy combines financial education workshops on topics like budgeting, banking, and credit with practical assistance navigating challenges like rental delinquency, utility arrears, and employment barriers.
Financial Management Support.activities:
Reaching and Investing in Youth for Sustainable Employment (RISE): In-school Youth (ISY) program
RISE: Out-of-school Youth (OSY) program
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program
Financial coaching
English Lessons Youth Mentoring Program
Goal Planning for Success (GPS) program

Workforce Development.label: Workforce Development
Workforce Development.details:
This strategy supports Riverside youth and adults in accessing sustainable employment that enables wealth building. For youth and young adults, RISE provides distinct pathways for in-school and out-of-school youth to gain employment skills and work experience. Both programs are designed to provide an eight week scaffolded, evidence-based curriculum of skill building developed by program specialists using individual student skill assessments to help students develop career and educational goals. This is followed by at least four weeks of work experience through externships (for in-school youth) or certifications (such as GEDs, for out-of-school youth). 

Through EMPOWER, adults and families receive ongoing case management services to meet their employment, financial, and additional goals. Programming includes employment-related trainings, workshops, personal coaching, and referrals to needed workforce resources that address barriers to employment and support job retention.  All workforce development programs include assessments to measure skill gains, provide individualized service strategies addressing participants' specific barriers, and track employment outcomes and retention to ensure participants not only obtain jobs but maintain employment over time.
Workforce Development.activities:
Economic Mobility Places Ownership Within Everyone's Reach (EMPOWER) program
Reaching and Investing in Youth for Sustainable Employment (RISE): In School Youth (ISY) program
RISE: Out of School Youth (OSY) program

Business and Entrepreneurship Developmen.label: Business and Entrepreneurship Development
Business and Entrepreneurship Developmen.details: This strategy provides step-by-step guidance for residents to transform business ideas into thriving enterprises through community partnerships. The program serves both aspiring entrepreneurs who need professional knowledge to develop comprehensive business strategies and existing business owners seeking to strengthen their operations and access new resources. Participants receive instruction on business plan fundamentals, market analysis, financial projections, marketing strategies, and legal structures, while connecting to resources including small business loans, technical assistance, and networking opportunities. By focusing on community-based business creation, the strategy builds local ownership of commercial enterprises that serve neighborhood needs and keep wealth circulating within the community.
